overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong, highly targeted social media presence across academic and professional circles on Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky. His content focuses heavily on computational social science, disinformation, and coordinated inauthentic behavior, attracting significant engagement from a niche audience of researchers and peers. While LinkedIn offers the largest absolute reach for milestones and recruitment, Bluesky delivers the highest engagement rate relative to follower count.

contentThemes.description: Announcements of new research publications, conference presentations, and software updates (CooRnet v2.0) targeting disinformation methods.
Hiring posts for postdoc researchers and PhD opportunities within his computational social science research group.
High-profile announcements detailing newly awarded research grants to study information operations.

recommendations: Capitalize on Bluesky's highly active audience by posting more frequent, interactive discussion threads regarding computational research methodologies.
Incorporate visual elements such as data visualizations, tool screenshots, or publication abstracts to increase visual engagement on text-heavy announcements.
Optimize recruitment posts by including call-to-actions, direct application links, and clear deadlines to convert high LinkedIn reach into applicants.

platformInsights.bluesky: Yields the highest average engagement rate (6.14%), indicating an exceptionally active community that responds strongly to publication and academic opportunity announcements.
platformInsights.mastodon: Provides a dedicated academic niche (aoir.social) well-suited for sharing specialized technical updates, like software releases, directly with fellow researchers.
platformInsights.linkedin: Serves as the primary driver of absolute interaction volume and reach, serving as the most effective channel for formal announcements, professional milestones, and job postings.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
